articles.title: State officials release 4 submissions to immigration agent misconduct portal launched in March
articles.description:
“We are reviewing and investigating these submissions as they come in and will work with the AG’s Office to release others when they are ready."

summary: State officials released 4 submissions to the immigration misconduct portal launched in March.
Investigations are ongoing, with collaboration from the Attorney General's Office.
Boston residents may see increased accountability for immigration agents through this initiative.

Boston has lost three of its last four.

articles.title: Weighing pros, cons of Celtics trading for Giannis Antetokounmpo
articles.description:
Antetokounmpo turns 32 years old in December, but the superstar forward could raise the Celtics' ceiling alongside Jayson Tatum.

summary: Celtics consider trading for Giannis Antetokounmpo to enhance team performance.

A trade could significantly impact Boston's chances for an NBA championship.

articles.title: Harvard College faculty to vote on limiting number of As for students
articles.description:
"Our grading is too compressed and too inflated, as nearly all faculty recognize; it is also too inconsistent, as students have observed."

summary: Harvard College faculty to vote on grading reforms on May 12, 2026.
Proposal aims to limit As to address grading inflation and inconsistency.
Changes could affect student GPAs and academic competitiveness in Boston.

articles.title: Truck driver dies days after crash on North Attleborough highway
articles.description:
Julio Flores from Pawtucket, Rhode Island, died on Monday nearly one week after a crash on I-95 South, the DA said.

The post Truck driver dies days after crash on North Attleborough highway appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Julio Flores, 45, from Pawtucket, died on May 8 after a crash on I-95 South.
The crash occurred on May 1, raising concerns about highway safety in the area.
Local residents may face increased traffic enforcement following this tragic incident.

articles.title: Former Celtic Jason Collins, NBA’s first openly gay player, dies at 47 of brain tumor
articles.description:
Collins had been diagnosed with Stage 4 glioblastoma, which has an extremely low survival rate. He was 47.

The post Former Celtic Jason Collins, NBA’s first openly gay player, dies at 47 of brain tumor appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Former Celtics player Jason Collins passed away at 47 due to a brain tumor.
Collins was diagnosed with Stage 4 glioblastoma, known for its low survival rate.
His legacy as the NBA's first openly gay player impacts Boston's LGBTQ+ community.

articles.title: Sports Hub’s Mike Felger shares wild Red Sox trade rumor involving Mike Trout

articles.title: Romeo Doubs on learning Josh McDaniels’ offense: ‘I can already see how he thinks’
articles.description:

summary: Romeo Doubs is adapting to Josh McDaniels’ offensive system with the New England Patriots.

Improved offensive strategies could enhance the Patriots' performance for Boston fans in the upcoming season.
